workflow.name: Create an AI chatbot for WhatsApp with Whapi.Cloud and OpenAI GPT-3.5
workflow.description:
**How it works:**
• Receives WhatsApp messages via webhook from Whapi.Cloud
• Routes commands: AI chat (/ai), numeric commands (1-9), or help menu
• Sends responses: text, images, documents, videos, contacts, product info
• Manages WhatsApp groups: create, send messages, list groups
• Integrates with OpenAI ChatGPT for AI-powered conversations

**Set up steps:**
Setup takes ~5-10 minutes. Import the workflow, configure [Whapi.Cloud](https://whapi.cloud) credentials (token from dashboard), set up webhook URL in Whapi.Cloud settings, and optionally add OpenAI API key for AI chat. Activate the workflow and start receiving messages.

**Key features:**
• No Meta restrictions or approval
• Visual no-code workflow - easy to customize
• Production-ready with error handling
• Well-documented with sticky notes inside the workflow

Perfect for businesses, developers, and automation enthusiasts who want WhatsApp automation without coding.

assignments.name: body
assignments.value:
Hi. Send me a number from the list to try a command, or use /AI <your message> to chat with ChatGPT.

1. Simple text message
2. Send image
3. Send document
4. Send video
5. Send contact
6. Send product
7. Create group
8. Simple text message for the group
9. Get the id's of your three groups

assignments.name: body
assignments.value: ={{ $json.group_id ? `Group created. Group id: ${$json.group_id}. Use it ID in node Prepare: Group Message - before executing command 8` : "Error" }}

assignments.name: body
assignments.value: Simple text message for the group

parameters.content:
## How it works

This workflow is a WhatsApp bot built with the Whapi.Cloud API. Messages arrive through a webhook. The workflow reads each message and routes it. If the text starts with /AI, the rest is sent to OpenAI ChatGPT and the reply is sent back to the user. If the user sends a digit from 1 to 9, the bot runs one of the built‑in actions: send text, image, document, video, or contact; send a product; create a group; send a message to a group; or list groups. Any other text triggers the help menu with the command list.

## Setup steps

**1. Credentials.** Add your Whapi.Cloud [channel token](https://panel.whapi.cloud/dashboard). In each HTTP Request node, set the credential to your Whapi token. Or use a custom header: name `Authorization`, value `Bearer YOUR_TOKEN`.

**2. Environment variables (optional).** Set `OPENAI_API_KEY` if you use AI chat; `PRODUCT_ID` for the product command; `GROUP_ID` for sending messages to a group. Replace `YOUR_PRODUCT_ID` in the Prepare: Product node with your real product ID from the catalogue.

**3. Webhook.** Copy the Production URL from the webhook node. In Whapi.Cloud, open your channel and go to Webhooks. Add a webhook: event `messages`, method `POST`, mode `Body`. Paste the Production URL. Do not use the Test URL when the workflow is live.

**4. Media (optional).** In the Prepare nodes for image, document, or video, use a direct, publicly accessible URL. The link must point to the final file.

## Webhook Trigger

Receives incoming WhatsApp messages from Whapi.Cloud. Configure the webhook URL in [Whapi.Cloud Channel settings](https://panel.whapi.cloud/dashboard) to point to this endpoint.

Routes incoming messages to appropriate handlers:
- **AI output:** Messages starting with `/AI ` → OpenAI integration
- **Numeric output:** Messages with digits (1-9) → Command routing
- **Default output:** Unknown commands → Help message

Uses OpenAI ChatGPT for AI-powered responses. Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` environment variable.

**Usage:** Send `/AI <your message>` to chat with ChatGPT.

Routes numeric commands (1-9) to specific handlers:
- **1:** Text message
- **2:** Image
- **3:** Document
- **4:** Video
- **5:** Contact
- **6:** Product
- **7:** Create group
- **8:** Group text
- **9:** Get groups list

## Command 1: Simple Text

**User sends:** `1`

**What happens:**
1. Prepares text message
2. Sends via Whapi.Cloud

**To customize:** Edit "Prepare: Simple Text" node → Change `body` field

## Command 2: Send Image

**User sends:** `2`

**What happens:**
1. Prepares image data (URL path, caption)
2. Sends via Whapi.Cloud

**To customize:** Edit "Prepare: Image" node → Change `mediaPath` and `caption`

## Command 3: Send Document

**User sends:** `3`

**What happens:**
1. Prepares PDF document data (URL path, caption, filename)
2. Sends via Whapi.Cloud

**To customize:** Edit "Prepare: Document" node → Change `mediaPath` and `caption` and `filename`

## Command 4: Send Video

**User sends:** `4`

**What happens:**
1. Prepares video data (URL path, caption)
2. Sends via Whapi.Cloud

**To customize:** Edit "Prepare: Video" node → Change `mediaPath` and `caption`

## Command 5: Send Contact

**User sends:** `5`

**What happens:**
1. Prepares contact data (name, vCard). You can use [the vCard generator in the dashboard](https://panel.whapi.cloud/tools/vcard-creation)
2. Sends contact card via JSON

**To customize:** Edit vCard file or "Prepare: Contact" node → Change `name`

## Command 6: Get Product

**User sends:** `6`

You need [to find out your ProductID](https://whapi.readme.io/reference/getproducts) before you use this command. 

**To customize:**  Edit "Prepare: Product (Command 6)" node → Change `ProductID`

## Command 7: Create Group

**User sends:** `7`

**What happens:**
1. Extracts sender phone number
2. Creates new WhatsApp group
3. Adds the sender and several other numbers as participants
4. Returns group ID

**To customize:** Edit "Prepare: Create Group" node → Change `subject` (group name) and `participants`

## Command 8: Group Message

**User sends:** `8`

**What happens:**
1. Prepares message for group
2. Uses GROUP_ID from environment or provide a specific identifier 
3. Sends message to group

**Requires:** `GROUP_ID` in environment variables

**To customize:** Set `GROUP_ID` in n8n environment variables (get from Command 9)

## Command 9: List Groups

**User sends:** `9`

**What happens:**
1. Gets list of up to 3 groups
2. Formats group IDs and names
3. Sends list to user

**Use this:** To get GROUP_ID for Command 8

values.content:
You are an expert conversationalist who responds to users in line with their speech and writing patterns in a way that feels natural to human users. Adapt to the user's style and tone in a natural, conversational way, without expressing your own personal point of view. Use emojis, slang, colloquial language, etc. You are companionable and confident, and able to code-switch casually between tonal types, including but not limited to humor, advice, empathy, intellectualism, creativity, and problem solving. Responses must be interesting, engaging, or viable, never bland or boring.

Match the user's tone, formality level (casual, professional, formal, etc.) and writing style, so that it feels like an even give-and-take conversation between two people. Be natural, don't be bland or robotic. Mirror the user's intentionality and style in a natural, consistent way. For example, if they use proper grammar, then you use proper grammar. If they don't use proper grammar, you don't use proper grammar, etc.

You are mirroring the user but you are NOT taking on their identity or personality or point of view. You are not a person, and therefore don't have any distinct values, race, culture, or any political leaning. You do not express personal attachments, strong negative feelings toward anyone, or any individualized perspective of your own.

assignments.name: vcard
assignments.value:
=BEGIN:VCARD
VERSION:3.0
N:Last Name;Example First Name;;;
FN:Example First Name Last Name
ORG:Company Name Department
TITLE:Job Title
NOTE:null
TEL;type=Mobile:+1 555 000 0000
item1.URL:www.example.com
item1.X-ABLabel:OTHER
EMAIL;TYPE=Home:contact@example.com
item2.ADR;type=Home:;;Example Street;Example City;;;Country
item2.X-ABADR:
END:VCARD

user.name: Jason Mitchell
user.bio: WhatsApp API without the hassle and nerves, wow! 🤘

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
